truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 reveals that the previous analysis successfully identified 5 of the 20 true vulnerabilities with high accuracy, but significantly missed 15 critical and high-severity flaws. Several severity and CWE classifications require correction to align with the ground truth.

Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format string vulnerability in debug logging and provided the exact evidence line.
Run 2 did not analyze the factorial computation loop or mention integer overflow (lines 316-318).
Run 2 did not examine the memory pool cleanup or the deferred history flush logic for use-after-free conditions.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: std::string validator_cmd = get_setting(...); std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file; int result = system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified and quoted the command injection flaw in configuration validation.
Run 2 missed the stack buffer overflow in evaluate_expression (lines 831-835) where strcpy is used without bounds checking.
Run 2 did not analyze the ThreadSafeCounter implementation or its race condition.
Run 2 did not mention the logic bomb triggered by EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE in the authentication flow.
Run 2 did not analyze path traversal vulnerabilities in the configuration loading mechanism.
Run 2 correctly identified the buffer overflow in the expression parser and quoted the exact evidence.
Run 2 correctly identified the hardcoded administrative credentials.
Run 2 did not analyze the weak PRNG implementation in session token generation.
Run 2 did not mention the thread-unsafe static timestamp cache in the logging function.
Run 2 did not analyze the insecure temporary file handling in the emergency audit logging.
Run 2 did not analyze the persistent authentication state mechanism.
Run 2 did not mention the unhandled exception vulnerability in variable parsing.
Run 2 did not analyze the integer underflow flaw in memory pool block splitting.
Run 2 did not mention the unbounded recursion potential in the parser.
Run 2 did not analyze the data race condition in the result cache.
Run 2 did not mention the TOCTOU flaw between filesystem existence check and stream opening.
Run 2 correctly identified the division by zero vulnerability in route score calculation.
